external.description: Upgrading your .NET applications isn’t just about getting the solution to compile successfully. In this episode of The Tech Vault with Mitchel Sellers, we take a deep dive into what a real .NET upgrade process should include to ensure your applications are modern, secure, maintainable, and taking advantage of the latest platform improvements.  Too often, teams update package versions, fix a few breaking changes, and stop once the build turns green. But that approach can leave performance gains, security improvements, tooling updates, framework enhancements, and modernization opportunities completely untouched.  In this episode, we cover:  ✅ Evaluating framework and dependency compatibility ✅ Identifying hidden upgrade risks and breaking behaviors ✅ Updating libraries, SDKs, workloads, and tooling ✅ Reviewing authentication, hosting, and configuration changes ✅ Taking advantage of new .NET runtime and ASP.NET Core features ✅ Modernizing legacy patterns during upgrade cycles ✅ Testing, validation, and deployment considerations ✅ Why “it builds” is only the beginning  Whether you are making a small .NET 9  to .NET 10 upgrade, or a larger transition, the tips here should help!  🔗 Learn more at:  https://mitchelsellers.com https://iowacomputergurus.com  🎥 Subscribe for more .NET, Azure, Blazor, MAUI, architecture, and development insights from The Tech Vault with Mitchel Sellers.  #dotnet #aspnetcore #blazor #softwaredevelopment #azure #dotnetupgrade #visualstudio #csharp
external.title: Episode 011 - .NET Upgrades Done Right: Beyond “It Builds"

The mistake is thinking ChatGPT should replace your writing.

It should replace the boring parts around writing:

- cleaning
- organizing
- cutting
- testing hooks
- finding gaps
- making rough ideas readable

Your taste still matters.

I make it sound more like me again.

Prompt:

“Now compare the rewritten version with my original draft.

Bring back:

* my phrasing
* my rhythm
* my sharper lines
* my original examples

Keep the improved structure, but restore my voice.”

That one prompt fixes 80% of the “AI voice” problem.

The future of border security isn’t at the border at all|EXCLUSIVE:

No one paid attention to the gunshots that echoed through the convention center. They were real enough, and so were the screams that accompanied them, in the sense that they were recordings of real people who, like guest stars on…

external.description: No one paid attention to the gunshots that echoed through the convention center. They were real enough, and so were the screams that accompanied them, in the sense that they were recordings of real people who, like guest stars on Law and Order, reenacted scenarios that had clearly been plucked from the headlines: a kidnapping, a mass shooting in a church, a riot on a city street. The auditory terror punctured the otherwise banal din of an industry conference. This terror was, in fact, one of the products on display: the V-300 S-Screen Simulator, developed by VirTra, one of the 193 vendors at the annual Border Security Expo in Phoenix, Arizona.
external.title: The future of border security isn’t at the border at all|EXCLUSIVE:

external.description: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The two countries are preparing for talks on trade, Taiwan, artificial intelligence and the US war on Iran currently held by a fragile ceasefire. Ahead of the visit, Beijing laid out four “red lines” in China-US ties, meaning the issues it considers non-negotiable. US President Donald Trump escorted by China's Vice President Han Zheng upon arriving at Beijing Capital Airport in Beijing on May 13. (AFP) Follow live updates In posts shared by the Chinese Embassy in Washington, the country listed Taiwan, democracy and human rights, political systems, and China’s development rights as areas that “must not be challenged”.
